tables.name: Antilag Start Delay
tables.description: time in seconds until antilag activates

tables.name: Enable Antilag Compressor Map and P-Fac Tables (during antilag)
tables.description:
enable: 1
disable : 0
Note: this is overriden by our custom boost control if the "Enable Custom WGDC Override" byte it set (in Custom Base Tables > WGDC).

Antilag Compressor Map with Required Compressor / Turbine Power - Air Mass Flow

KnockCEL Load Threshold (Minimum)
tables.description: knockCEL is only active above this load

tables.name: KnockCEL Pedal Threshold (Minimum)
tables.description: knockCEL is only active above this pedal percentage

tables.name: KnockCEL Single Cyl Total
tables.description: single cyl's timing corrections to trigger knockCEL

tables.name: KnockCEL Sum Total
tables.description: sum of each cyl's timing corrections to trigger knockCEL

tables.name: Boost Ceiling (Relative) (Gear x RPM) (Map 1)
tables.description: MHD+ custom table that replaces the single cell Boost Ceiling (when enable byte is set in MHD+ Config). Units are PSI, relative. This ceiling is applied to the intake manifold boost target.  TMAP boost target is derived from this by multiplying it by the factor output from KF_PSPLD_ECO / DYN tables.

tables.name: Boost Ceiling (Relative) (Gear x RPM) (Map 2)
tables.description: MHD+ custom table that replaces the single cell Boost Ceiling (when enable byte is set in MHD+ Config). Units are PSI, relative. This ceiling is applied to the intake manifold boost target.  TMAP boost target is derived from this by multiplying it by the factor output from KF_PSPLD_ECO / DYN tables.

tables.name: Boost Ceiling (Relative) (Gear x RPM) (Map 3)
tables.description: MHD+ custom table that replaces the single cell Boost Ceiling (when enable byte is set in MHD+ Config). Units are PSI, relative. This ceiling is applied to the intake manifold boost target.  TMAP boost target is derived from this by multiplying it by the factor output from KF_PSPLD_ECO / DYN tables.

tables.name: Boost Ceiling (Relative) (Gear x RPM) (Map 4)
tables.description: MHD+ custom table that replaces the single cell Boost Ceiling (when enable byte is set in MHD+ Config). Units are PSI, relative. This ceiling is applied to the intake manifold boost target.  TMAP boost target is derived from this by multiplying it by the factor output from KF_PSPLD_ECO / DYN tables.

tables.name: Boost Ceiling (Relative) (Gear x RPM) (FF)
tables.description: MHD+ custom table that replaces the single cell Boost Ceiling (when enable byte is set in MHD+ Config). Units are PSI, relative. This ceiling is applied to the intake manifold boost target.  TMAP boost target is derived from this by multiplying it by the factor output from KF_PSPLD_ECO / DYN tables.

tables.name: Boost Ceiling (Relative) (Gear x RPM) (FF#2)
tables.description: MHD+ custom table that replaces the single cell Boost Ceiling (when enable byte is set in MHD+ Config). Units are PSI, relative. This ceiling is applied to the intake manifold boost target.  TMAP boost target is derived from this by multiplying it by the factor output from KF_PSPLD_ECO / DYN tables.

Active Map Slots
tables.description: max number of map slots you wish to be selectable

tables.name: Enable Custom 3D Boost Ceiling Table
tables.description: Setting to 1 uses the custom MHD+ Gear x RPM boost ceiling tables instead of the OEM single cell table.

tables.name: Enable Individual FF#2 Blending
tables.description:
Setting to 1 will blend from map slot to FF#2 for ipol factors greater than 1.0.  Any other value keeps the default functionality where ipol factors greater than 1.0 blend from FF to FF#2.

Example with toggle set to 0:
ipol factors 0 - 1 blend from map slot to FF1
ipol factors 1.01 - 2 blend from FF1 to FF2

Example with toggle set to 1:
ipol factors 0 - 1 blend from map slot to FF1
ipol factors 1.01 - 2 blend from map slot to FF2

tables.name: Static Ethanol Content (Map 1)
tables.description: Setting to a value > 0 will override (or substitute) the E% value coming from your CANBUS ECA.

tables.name: Static Ethanol Content (Map 2)
tables.description: Setting to a value > 0 will override (or substitute) the E% value coming from your CANBUS ECA.

tables.name: Static Ethanol Content (Map 3)
tables.description: Setting to a value > 0 will override (or substitute) the E% value coming from your CANBUS ECA.

tables.name: Static Ethanol Content (Map 4)
tables.description: Setting to a value > 0 will override (or substitute) the E% value coming from your CANBUS ECA.

tables.name: Start and Warmup Enrichment (Ethanol Compensation)
tables.description: The factor output of this table is applied (multiplied) to fuel at start, warmup enrichment, and hot restart automatically for map slots that are either FlexFuel enabled or have a static ethanol content set.

tables.name: Start and Warmup Enrichment (Ethanol Compensation) - Tmot
Start and Warmup Enrichment (Ethanol Compensation) - Ethanol Content
Enable Custom WGDC Override
tables.description: Setting to 1 will override the base WGDC % with the value from our MHD+ Custom WGDC Base table

tables.name: Enable Custom WGDC P and D Factors
tables.description: Setting to 1 will apply the output of our MHD+ Custom WGDC P and D factor tables to WGDC base.

tables.name: WGDC P-Factor (Custom)
tables.description: Output of this table gets added to (or subtracted from) WGDC Base % when then custom factor enable flag is set

tables.name: WGDC D-Factor (Custom)
tables.description: Output of this table gets added to (or subtracted from) WGDC Base % when then custom factor enable flag is set

tables.name: ReFlex Aux 2 Pedal Threshold (Minimum w/ hysteresis)
tables.description: Aux 2 duty cycle output will be enabled when pedal is great than the ON threshold, and once active, will not be disabled until it falls below the OFF threshold.

tables.name: ReFlex Aux 3 Pedal Threshold (Minimum w/ hysteresis)
tables.description: Aux 3 duty cycle output will be enabled when pedal is great than the ON threshold, and once active, will not be disabled until it falls below the OFF threshold.

tables.name: ReFlex Aux 4 Pedal Threshold (Minimum w/ hysteresis)
tables.description: Aux 4 duty cycle output will be enabled when pedal is great than the ON threshold, and once active, will not be disabled until it falls below the OFF threshold.

tables.name: ReFlex Aux 2 Set as Relay Trigger
tables.description: Set to 1 if this aux output is triggering a relay. It will then only output DC% at either 0 or 100% (0-49.9% treated as 0%, 50-100% treated as 100%)

tables.name: ReFlex Aux 3 Set as Relay Trigger
tables.description: Set to 1 if this aux output is triggering a relay. It will then only output DC% at either 0 or 100% (0-49.9% treated as 0%, 50-100% treated as 100%)

tables.name: ReFlex Aux 4 Set as Relay Trigger
tables.description: Set to 1 if this aux output is triggering a relay. It will then only output DC% at either 0 or 100% (0-49.9% treated as 0%, 50-100% treated as 100%)

tables.name: Enable Expanded ReFlex Integration and Control
tables.description: Set this to 1 to enable expanded integration (for extra sensor inputs like 2x EGT, one more pressure input (for coolant or crankcase pressure, EGBP, etc), and expanded aux pressure range). Requires ReFlex to be updated to at least firmware v7.50? 8.00?

tables.name: Enable DME Control of ReFlex WGDC
Enable DME Control of ReFlex Aux 2 Output
Enable DME Control of ReFlex Aux 3 Output (ReFlex+ only!)
Enable DME Control of ReFlex Aux 4 Output (ReFlex+ only!)
Enable DME Control of ReFlex Port Injection
tables.description:
00: Disabled (Calibrate PI directly on the ReFlex)
01: Manual (Calibrate using MHD+ IPW tables equivalent to tuning on the ReFlex directly)
02: Auto (Calibrate with accurate port injector characteristics and PI / DI split tables)
99: Calculation Output (special mode for logging / validation only)

00 and 01 require you to reduce DI yourself via the OEM / Mapswitch Fuel Scalar tables.

02 (Auto mode) will automatically lower the DI fuel mass to account for the PI fuel mass being injected. The OEM / Mapswitch Fuel Scalar tables should be set as if the vehicle is running DI only.

tables.name: ReFlex FlexFuel Sensor Absent
tables.description: Set this to 1 if you do NOT have an ethanol sensor wired to your ReFlex.  This will prevent the MHD005 error code and CEL entirely.

tables.name: ReFlex Aux 2 Output Interpolation (Map 1)
tables.description: FlexFuel interpolation blend factor for the MHD+ ReFlex aux 2 output tables

tables.name: ReFlex Aux 2 Output Interpolation (Map 2)
tables.description: FlexFuel interpolation blend factor for the MHD+ ReFlex aux 2 output tables

tables.name: ReFlex Aux 2 Output Interpolation (Map 3)
tables.description: FlexFuel interpolation blend factor for the MHD+ ReFlex aux 2 output tables

tables.name: ReFlex Aux 2 Output Interpolation (Map 4)
tables.description: FlexFuel interpolation blend factor for the MHD+ ReFlex aux 2 output tables

WGDC Base (Custom) (Antilag / Custom LC) - Boost Target
WGDC P-Factor (Custom) (Antilag / Custom LC)
tables.description: Output of this table gets added to (or subtracted from) WGDC Base % when then custom factor enable flag is set (during antilag and our custom launch control)

tables.name: WGDC P-Factor (Custom) (Antilag / Custom LC) - Boost Deviation
WGDC D-Factor (Custom) (Antilag / Custom LC)
tables.description: Output of this table gets added to (or subtracted from) WGDC Base % when then custom factor enable flag is set (during antilag and our custom launch control)

tables.name: WGDC D-Factor (Custom) (Antilag / Custom LC) - Boost Deviation
WGDC D-Factor (Custom) (Antilag / Custom LC) - Boost Deviation Gradient
Inhibit Mapswitch Controls
tables.description: Set to 1 to keep wheel controls active for antilag only

tables.name: NLS RPM Threshold (Minimum)
tables.description: NLS logic will only activate above this RPM

tables.name: NLS Pedal Threshold (Minimum)
tables.description: NLS logic will only activate above this pedal percentage

tables.name: Enable Port Injection Safety
tables.description: Set to 1 to enable; Will cut each cylinder's coil when the corresponding injector is cut.

tables.name: Disable Cold Start
tables.description: Set to 1 to fully disable cold start for that map slot. Any other value is treated as 0, and will use OEM tables to determine if cold start is used.

tables.name: TCU TQ Limit Override
tables.description:
01: Ignore TCU TQ limit always
02: Ignore TCU TQ limit except during launch control (after moving and launch gear [1st or 2nd] fully engaged)
03: Ignore TCU TQ limit except during launch control (after launch traction mode ends, usually end of 2nd or 3rd gear).
NOTE: Setting 3 should NOT be used on S58 due to their special launch control. We recommend using setting 2 (or 4 with xHP)
04: No Override - TCU TQ limit fully active

tables.name: Enable Torque per Gear Tables
tables.description: Set to 1 to enable use of the Maximum Clutch Torque per Gear tables. (make sure to also fill base table in Limits folder!)

tables.name: Enable Single Bank (S58 only)
tables.description: For S58 single turbo cars only (has no effect on others)!  You must have the bank 1 primary O2 sensor moved to the downpipe (close to turbine exit), and the bank 2 primary sensor removed. Any bungs in the manifold should be plugged.

tables.name: Enable Mapswitch for Perf. Gauge Tables
Low Pressure Fuel Sensor Source (General)
tables.description:
00: Static Substitute Value (boost referenced FPR required! otherwise engine damage can occur)
01: OEM Sensor Value (S58 only)
02: Motiv ReFlex Aux Pressure

NOTE: Even with a boost referenced FPR, using a pressure substitute value is not optimal. A physical low pressure sensor is always recommended.

tables.name: Base Fuel Pressure (Low Pressure) (General)
tables.description: Low pressure fuel system normal operating pressure. This is directly correlated with Injector Scalar on injector calibration data sheets. If using option 0 for lpfp sensor source (above), this value will also be used as the static substitute value for low pressure.

tables.name: Injector Scalar (Auto)
tables.description:
Port fuel injector fuel in cc/min. Make sure to enter this appropriately for your base pressure.

Required for auto PI strategy.

NOTE::: Do NOT use WinOLS to enter this table value, it will be incorrect as the scaling conversion format is not supported.

tables.name: Differential Fuel Pressure Compensation (Auto) - Differential Pressure Error Percent
Non-Linear Injector Compensation Maximum IPW (Auto)
tables.description: The factor from the 3D Non-Linear Injector Compensation table will be applied up until this effective IPW. Required for auto PI strategy.

tables.name: Non-Linear Injector Compensation (Auto)
tables.description: Factor applied to effective IPW. This corrects for the inherent non-linearity in small pulse widths. Required for auto PI strategy.

tables.name: Non-Linear Injector Compensation (Auto) - Injector Pulse Width
Non-Linear Injector Compensation (Auto) - Differential Fuel Pressure
PI / DI Percent Split (Gasoline) (Auto)
tables.description:
This defines what percentage of the total fuel mass the port injection system will be responsible for (with gasoline). Max allowed is 85%.
This is blended with the Ethanol version of this table (when FlexFuel or Static E content is being used for the current map slot) using the factor from the Port Injection Interpolation table.

Required for auto PI strategy.

tables.name: PI / DI Percent Split (Ethanol) (Auto)
tables.description:
This defines what percentage of the total fuel mass the port injection system will be responsible for (with ethanol). Max allowed is 85%.
This is blended with the Ethanol version of this table (when FlexFuel or Static E content is being used for the current map slot) using the factor from the Port Injection Interpolation table.

Required for auto PI strategy.

tables.name: Maximum Allowed DI Usage (Auto)
tables.description:
This is the maximum fuel mass (mg/stk) you want the DI system to handle. If the resulting DI fuel mass after the PI/DI split is greater than this amount, it will be added to the PI split. Use this table as an added safety to avoid maxing the DI injectors / HPFP.

Required for auto PI strategy.

tables.name: Maximum Allowed DI Usage (Auto) - Ethanol Content
Port Injection Interpolation (General)
tables.description: FlexFuel blend factor used to interpolate between the PI / DI split gasoline and ethanol tables or the Manual IPW gasoline and ethanol tables, depending on the enabled PI strategry.

tables.name: Port Injection Interpolation (General) - Ethanol Content
tables.description: The Ethanol Content (Active) logging parameter is used for this axis lookup.

tables.name: Minimum Injector Pulsewidth (General)
tables.description: Required by auto and manual PI strategies.

tables.name: PI Injector Pulse Width (Gasoline) (Manual)
tables.description:
This is the Injector Pulse Width (IPW) sent to the port injection system (with gasoline).

Required for manual PI strategy.

tables.name: PI Injector Pulse Width (Ethanol) (Manual)
tables.description:
This is the Injector Pulse Width (IPW) sent to the port injection system (with ethanol).

Required for manual PI strategy.

tables.name: Low Pressure Fuel Pressure Factor (Manual)
tables.description: Multiplicative factor applied to the Manual IPW table result (after ethanol interpolation). Required for manual PI strategy.

tables.name: Low Pressure Fuel Pressure Factor (Manual) - Low Pressure Fuel Pressure
Enable Increased Boost Ceiling (5 bar+)
tables.description: Set to 1 to enable our custom patch which allows full use of 5 bar (and higher) MAP sensors. If using the OEM sensor gradient and offset tables, the following PnP Bosch 5 bar map sensor is required (part #: 13628514488)!  Otherwise you will need to enable and use the custom MAP scaling tables we provide.

tables.name: MHD+ Load Calculation Config
tables.description:
0 - disabled, no correction
1 - correct Load Actual (relative filling model)
2 - correct Load Actual (relative filling model) and also enable Load Reduction Scaling

Please read our MHD+ Suite tuning guide PDF for more information.

tables.name: Load Reduction Scaling
tables.description:
Use this table to stay under the 327% max load ceiling at higher boost levels. A factor of 1.0 is "no reduction", while lower factors will scale load down proportionally so that higher boost levels will not all trace the same 327% column. Fuel mass is automatically corrected for this as well.

Used when MHD+ Load Calculation Config is set to 2.

NOTE: Smoothness of table z-data is important! Please read our MHD+ Suite tuning guide PDF for more information.

tables.name: Enable Custom MAP Sensor Scaling
tables.description: Set to 1 to use our custom MAP sensor scaling table instead of the OEM gradient and offset tables. This is only for the pre-throttle sensor.

tables.name: Pre-Throttle MAP Sensor Scaling (Custom)
tables.description: This table can be used to either gain a bit of sensor resolution by using some of it's non-linear range, or to support a sensor that would not work in the OEM gradient/offset tables due to hex maxes.

tables.name: LPFP Safety Load Threshold (Minimum)
tables.description: Safety is only monitored once load actual is above this threshold.

tables.name: LPFP Safety Pedal Threshold (Minimum)
tables.description: Safety is only monitored once pedal percentage is above this threshold.

tables.name: LPFP Safety RPM Threshold (Minimum)
tables.description: Safety is only monitored once RPM is above this threshold.

tables.name: LPFP Safety Min/Max
tables.description: Safety Reaction is triggered if the value from Safety Input Source exceeds these bounds longer than Safety Delay Time.

tables.name: LPFP Safety Input Source
tables.description:
0: LPFP Actual (from Port Injection configuration)
1: Differential Fuel Pressure

NOTE: Both settings require a physical Low Pressure Fuel Sensor!

tables.name: LPFP Safety Reaction Config
tables.description:
0: Apply configured boost / load limits (no CEL)
1: Apply configured boost / load limits (with CEL)
2: Trigger full MHD+ limp mode

tables.name: LPFP Safety Reaction Load Limit
tables.description: Load request is limited to a maximum of this value while safety is triggered.

tables.name: LPFP Safety Reaction Boost Limit
tables.description: Boost target is limited to a maximum of this value while safety is triggered.

tables.name: LPFP Safety Reaction Disable Aux Outputs
tables.description: Set to 1 to selectively disable Aux Outputs (0% DC) while safety is triggered.

tables.name: LPFP Safety Reaction Delay Time
tables.description: Safety Reaction is triggered if the value from Safety Input Source exceeds its bounds for longer than this duration.

tables.name: LPFP Safety Reaction Heal Time
tables.description:
Safety Reaction is removed if the value from Safety Input Source returns within bounds (or main load/pedal/RPM thresholds no longer met) for longer than this duration.

NOTE: A duration of 0 sec here prevents healing and requires clearing codes or restarting the vehicle to remove the triggered safety reaction.

tables.name: Aux 2 Safety Load Threshold (Minimum)
tables.description: Safety is only monitored once load actual is above this threshold.

tables.name: Aux 2 Safety Pedal Threshold (Minimum)
tables.description: Safety is only monitored once pedal percentage is above this threshold.

tables.name: Aux 2 Safety RPM Threshold (Minimum)
tables.description: Safety is only monitored once RPM is above this threshold.

tables.name: Aux 2 Safety Min/Max
tables.description: Safety Reaction is triggered if the value from Safety Input Source exceeds these bounds longer than Safety Delay Time.

tables.name: Aux 2 Safety Input Source
tables.description: 0: ReFlex Aux 2 pressure sensor (example use cases: coolant pressure, exhaust gas back pressure, crankcase pressure, etc)

tables.name: Aux 2 Safety Reaction Config
tables.description:
0: Apply configured boost / load limits (no CEL)
1: Apply configured boost / load limits (with CEL)
2: Trigger full MHD+ limp mode

tables.name: Aux 2 Safety Reaction Load Limit
tables.description: Load request is limited to a maximum of this value while safety is triggered.

tables.name: Aux 2 Safety Reaction Boost Limit
tables.description: Boost target is limited to a maximum of this value while safety is triggered.

tables.name: Aux 2 Safety Reaction Disable Aux Outputs
tables.description: Set to 1 to selectively disable Aux Outputs (0% DC) while safety is triggered.

tables.name: Aux 2 Safety Reaction Delay Time
tables.description: Safety Reaction is triggered if the value from Safety Input Source exceeds its bounds for longer than this duration.

tables.name: Aux 2 Safety Reaction Heal Time
tables.description:
Safety Reaction is removed if the value from Safety Input Source returns within bounds (or main load/pedal/RPM thresholds no longer met) for longer than this duration.

NOTE: A duration of 0 sec here prevents healing and requires clearing codes or restarting the vehicle to remove the triggered safety reaction.

tables.name: EGT Safety Load Threshold (Minimum)
tables.description: Safety is only monitored once load actual is above this threshold.

tables.name: EGT Safety Pedal Threshold (Minimum)
tables.description: Safety is only monitored once pedal percentage is above this threshold.

tables.name: EGT Safety RPM Threshold (Minimum)
tables.description: Safety is only monitored once RPM is above this threshold.

tables.name: EGT Safety Min/Max
tables.description: Safety Reaction is triggered if the value from Safety Input Source exceeds these bounds longer than Safety Delay Time.

tables.name: EGT Safety Input Source
tables.description:
0: ReFlex Bank 1 EGT
1: ReFlex Bank 2 EGT
2: AVG of ReFlex Bank 1 and 2 EGT;
3: MIN/MAX of ReFlex Bank 1 and 2 EGT applied respectively to each safety bound.

tables.name: EGT Safety Reaction Config
tables.description:
0: Apply configured boost / load limits (no CEL)
1: Apply configured boost / load limits (with CEL)
2: Trigger full MHD+ limp mode

tables.name: EGT Safety Reaction Load Limit
tables.description: Load request is limited to a maximum of this value while safety is triggered.

tables.name: EGT Safety Reaction Boost Limit
tables.description: Boost target is limited to a maximum of this value while safety is triggered.

tables.name: EGT Safety Reaction Disable Aux Outputs
tables.description: Set to 1 to selectively disable Aux Outputs (0% DC) while safety is triggered.

tables.name: EGT Safety Reaction Delay Time
tables.description: Safety Reaction is triggered if the value from Safety Input Source exceeds its bounds for longer than this duration.

tables.name: EGT Safety Reaction Heal Time
tables.description:
Safety Reaction is removed if the value from Safety Input Source returns within bounds (or main load/pedal/RPM thresholds no longer met) for longer than this duration.

NOTE: A duration of 0 sec here prevents healing and requires clearing codes or restarting the vehicle to remove the triggered safety reaction.

tables.name: Enable Simplified Rev Limiter
tables.description: Set to 01 to use our custom rev limiter table.

tables.name: Disable Torque Limiter 4096 (VMAX)
tables.description: Set to 1 to fully disable this torque limiter.

tables.name: Disable Torque Limiter 8192 (ACCEL)
tables.description: Set to 1 to fully disable this torque limiter.

tables.name: Disable Torque Limiter 2097152 (MD_RAD)
tables.description: Set to 1 to fully disable this torque limiter.

tables.name: Disable Torque Limiter 8388608 (MT Clutch Slip)
tables.description: Set to 1 to fully disable this torque limiter.

tables.name: Sensor Preset Selection
tables.description:
00: Disabled / Not Used
01: MHD CANBUS ECA
02: Motiv ReFlex
03: Zeitronix CANBUS ECA

tables.name: Sensor Installed on PT-CAN2
tables.description: Set to 01 only if this sensor is wired to PT-CAN2. Otherwise 0.

tables.name: Ethanol Content Substitute Value (Error)
tables.description: this will be used as the ethanol percent value if the CANBUS signal times out or the sensor reports an error / water contamination

tables.name: Ethanol Content Hold (RPM Threshold)
tables.description:
Lock the Ethanol Content (Active) reading above this RPM threshold. Useful on very high power builds / high flow fuel systems.
Values <= 0 are treated as "no lock".

tables.name: Ethanol Content Hold (Load Threshold)
tables.description:
Lock the Ethanol Content (Active) reading above this Load threshold. Useful on very high power builds / high flow fuel systems.
Values <= 0 are treated as "no lock".

tables.name: ReFlex Aux Pressure Sensor Safety (Minimum)
tables.description: will trigger a limp mode if the aux pressure data from ReFlex falls below this value as a safety

tables.name: Codeword for MHD+ Errors (Inhibit Limp)
tables.description: Ignore this, use the separate flags above.
